changes.value:
## What
Move the `execute_operation` function from `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs:382-804` into `swissarmyhammer-kanban` as a shared public function. This eliminates dispatch duplication between the MCP tool and the new kanban-cli.

**Create:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`
- `pub async fn dispatch_operation(ctx: &KanbanContext, op: &Operation) -> Result<Value, KanbanError>`
- Same match logic as existing `execute_operation`, but returns `KanbanError` instead of `McpError`
- Convert `McpError::invalid_params("missing required field: X")` â†’ `KanbanError::MissingField("X")` (or use `op.require_string()` which already returns `Result<&str, String>`)

**Modify:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs`
- Add `pub mod dispatch;`
- Re-export: `pub use dispatch::dispatch_operation;`

**Modify:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs`
- Replace inline `execute_operation` with a thin wrapper that calls `swissarmyhammer_kanban::dispatch_operation()` and maps `KanbanError` â†’ `McpError`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dispatch_operation` lives in `swissarmyhammer-kanban` crate, usable without MCP dependencies
- [ ] MCP kanban tool still works identically (passes all existing tests)
- [ ] `KanbanError` variant covers "missing required field" cases (or reuse existing variant)

## Tests
- [ ] Existing MCP kanban tool tests pass unchanged: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- kanban`
- [ ] New unit test in dispatch.rs: init board + add task + list tasks round-trip
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` passes

+## What
Add a generic `OperationRegistry<C, E>` to `swissarmyhammer-operations` that maps `(verb, noun)` â†’ deserialize + execute. Then build a kanban-specific registry in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`. This eliminates the ~420-line dispatch match block in the MCP tool and prevents duplication in kanban-cli.

### Part A: Generic registry in `swissarmyhammer-operations`

**Create:** `swissarmyhammer-operations/src/registry.rs`
- `OperationRegistry<C, E>` with a `HashMap<(&str, &str), BoxedHandler>`
- `register<T: DeserializeOwned + Execute<C, E>>()` â€” standard registration: deserializes params JSON into `T`, calls `processor.process(&t, ctx)`
- `register_with_hook<T, F>(hook: F)` â€” registration with a pre-processing closure that can modify params before deserialization (for the 3 custom cases)
- `dispatch(ctx, processor, verb, noun, params) -> Result<Value, E>` â€” look up handler, run it
- Also expose `operation_metadata()` returning `&dyn Operation` instances for CLI generation

**Modify:** `swissarmyhammer-operations/src/lib.rs` â€” add `pub mod registry;`, re-export

### Part B: Kanban registry in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`

**Create:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dispatch.rs`
- `pub fn kanban_registry() -> OperationRegistry<KanbanContext, KanbanError>`
- Registers all ~30 operations using plain `register` 
- Registers `AddTask`, `AddTag`, `AddActor` with hooks for: auto-assign actor, name/id fallback, ensure flag
- `pub async fn dispatch_operation(ctx, op) -> Result<Value, KanbanError>` convenience function

**Modify:** `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs` â€” add `pub mod dispatch;`, re-export

### Part C: Wire up MCP tool

**Modify:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/kanban/mod.rs`
- Replace the 420-line `execute_operation` match with a call to `kanban_registry().dispatch()`
- Keep the plan notification logic and actor injection as pre-processing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `OperationRegistry` is generic and lives in `swissarmyhammer-operations` (no kanban dependency)
- [ ] All ~30 kanban operations registered, including 3 with hooks
- [ ] MCP kanban tool uses the registry â€” no more manual dispatch match
- [ ] All existing MCP kanban tests pass unchanged

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-operations`: registry with test operations, dispatch round-trip
- [ ] Unit test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/dispatch.rs`: init board + add task + list via registry
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-operations` passes
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- kanban` passes
\ No newline at end of file
